body: She said it and I had to put my coffee down.    \\Two million dollars\‚\\" I said.    \\"Two million\‚\\" she confirmed.    \\"To figure out why nurses are miserable.\\"    \\"And what did the consulting firm say?\\" I asked.    \\"I don\\'t know yet\‚\\" she said.    \\"The report isn\\'t done.\\"    \\"But I can tell you for free\‚\\" she said.    \\"We\\'re exhausted. We\\'re understaffed. We\\'re underpaid.\\"    \\"And nobody at the top actually listens to us.\\"    \\"There\‚\\" she said.    \\"That\\'s the two million dollar report.\\"    \\"Save the check.\\"    I almost laughed.    But she wasn\\'t being funny.    She was being precise.    \\"How long have you been in the ER?\\" I asked.    \\"Eleven years\‚\\" she said.    \\"And in eleven years\‚\\" I asked\‚ \\"has it gotten better or worse?\\"    She didn\\'t even pause.    \\"Worse\‚\\" she said.    \\"Every year. Without exception.\\"    \\"More patients. Less staff. Same speech from administration about doing more with less.\\"    \\"And the lunch breaks?\\" I asked.    She shrugged.    \\"I eat standing up at the nurses station\‚\\" she said.    \\"If I eat at all.\\"    I looked at her for a long moment.    \\"You are the most resilient kind of person I know\‚\\" I said.    \\"And you deserve to be building something that is resilient for you.\\"    \\"Not just surviving a system that keeps proving it won\\'t change.\\"    She was quiet.    \\"What does that look like?\\" she asked.    \\"It looks like ownership\‚\\" I said.    \\"Of your time. Your income. Your future.\\"    \\"And none of it requires a $2 million consulting report to figure out.\\"    Comment FREEDOM and I\\'ll show you what building your own thing looks like for nurses who are done waiting for the system to fix itself."

body: The problem isn’t that we don’t understand why survivors won’t leave abuse. The problem is we don’t understand perpetrators and what survivors are facing. If we understood that\‚ whatever the survivor is doing would make perfect sense. They deserve respect for surviving - not judgement for how they survived.    It’s insulting to say that survivors need to ‘find the courage to leave’. Courage is not the problem. Survivors already have more courage than most ever will and certainly more than they should’ve ever had to have. The problem is that survivors know very well the reality of their options.     Shaming those entrapped in abusive situations that they don’t have the ‘courage to tell’ is incredibly harmful. What would help is honoring what the survivor is facing\‚ and making sure if they take that risk to tell or leave\‚ that they WILL be protected.    🤍 Like and Save and Share to spread this message    ✨Link in bio to download “13 Tips For Parents and Caregivers”  ✨Link in bio for our YouTube video on courage  ✨Link in bio to shop empowering merch  ✨Link in bio for our Substack    ♡ follow @defendsurvivors (Shares and saves are appreciated. Please do not repost)  #defendsurvivors #respectsurvivors #whyididntreport #courage #abuseawareness

body: Why didn\\'t you report it?    (IG only let\\'s you post 3 minutes... the rest of this video is on the ⏰️ app)    Legal protection against DA/DV/Marital didn\\'t exist until 1994.    I turned 9 that year. This didn\\'t exist until during my lifetime. Meaning... There wasn\\'t a whole lot of understanding about what you needed for evidence.    That.. and verbal/emotional abvse are much more difficult to prove. (Not to mention the million & one legal loopholes there are to get evidence blocked in court- but that\\'s an entirely different story).    I often had people ask why my mom didn\\'t leave sooner... & now I have an answer. Because by the time it was even legal.. I was almost 9. I had 3 or 4 siblings. It\\'s not that easy to just leave.    As for myself... In the end I did. But it wasn\\'t that easy & he followed me from CA back home to WI & would park in my driveway.    10 years later I still have PTSD.    But I didn\\'t report it because I just wanted to get away. And I didn\\'t know if it would help or make it worse when I was leaving.    & as a woman\‚ I have no idea what being in a relationship would be like in the future.. because protections are going away for queer folk\‚ and women\‚ so no matter who I chose to be in a relationship with .. if it\\'s a woman\‚ we lose protections for us\‚ and if it\\'s a man... in the next four years will we lose the protection against DV?    It\\'s a rather unsettling time to be a woman... Especially as a mother of girls.    #domesticabuseawareness  #domesticviolenceawareness  #domesticabusesurvivor #domesticabuse #domesticviolencesurvivor #domesticviolence #ptsd #ptsdawareness #trauma #traumahealing #traumasurvivor  #divorced #igotout #runlikeagirl

body: I told my younger self It\\'s alright to leave 🕊️     And I want to watch her grow.    ✨TW - SA    I\\'ve alluded to it\‚ but I\\'ve only ever said it quietly  In small spaces   The right people  Shame   Hiding his wrong doings   Thinking it makes me a bad person  Silently pushing through  Not going to the police  Protecting my family  Until I snapped and couldn\\'t take another day  I was 16 and finally had enough of the madness    I was 7  I was 8  I was 9  .......... You get it     I grew up in a home with an abusive alcoholic  I slept with the cordless phone under my pillow  I called the cops a lot   I didn\\'t sleep much  I slept on a couch in an unfinished basement  I had him successfully imprisoned once  I protected my mom and brother  I screamed a lot  I cried less than I screamed    I\\'m a sexual assault victim and survivor  Finding my way through life   Breaking down one trauma response at a time     If you\\'ve ever thought I was abrasive or tough or a bitch  I was fighting for my life and I\\'m learning  Learning to feel safe and loved   Learning to stand up for myself\‚ softer  Learning that I can move beyond shame  Learning that it wasn\\'t my fault    I know there\\'s stats about how frequently people suffer from SA\‚ and its high. But the reality is\‚ the statistic is low because not everyone reports.   I\\'m still fighting with what to do about that.    I try to show up as authentically as I can in this fucked up lil world and if me being me helps just one person\‚ then it\\'s worth it.     You\\'re not alone and you don\\'t have to be.    Walking alongside you\‚   T✨

body: Bluma Zeigarnik published her findings in 1927\‚ but the effect wasn\\'t taken seriously until the 1960s when cognitive psychology proved she was right: incomplete tasks occupy 90% more mental space than completed ones.    Here\\'s What She Didn\\'t Know (But We Do Now):    The effect works because of how your brain allocates attention. When you start a task\‚ your brain creates a \\tension state\\" – essentially a reminder loop that keeps firing until the task is complete. Finishing it releases dopamine and closes the loop.     Not finishing it?   The loop stays open\‚ draining cognitive resources.    📊 The Dark Side:   This is why you can\\'t relax when you have unfinished work. Your brain literally won\\'t let you. Studies show people with multiple incomplete tasks report 32% higher stress levels and worse sleep quality.    💡 But Here\\'s the Exploit:    Zeigarnik\\'s effect isn\\'t just about memory – it\\'s about compulsion.   Marketing teams realized if they can get you to start something (a show\‚ a game\‚ a course)\‚ your brain will obsess until you finish it. That\\'s why:  Free trials work better than discounts (you\\'ve already started)    \\"Chapter 1 FREE\\" hooks readers harder than reviews  Duolingo\\'s streak counter is more addictive than the lessons    🎯 The Counterintuitive Truth:    Zeigarnik herself struggled with this discovery. She couldn\\'t turn off her own research. Knowing about the effect doesn\\'t make you immune – it makes you more aware of how easily you\\'re manipulated.    The waiters in that café weren\\'t superhuman.   They were trapped in cognitive loops.    Just like you are right now\‚ wondering how this caption ends... 🔄  .  #marketing #psychology #businessgrowth #business #fact"

body: Conversion therapy didn’t come from Jesus. It came from outdated psychology and fear based theology.    It was built on the idea that people needed to be “fixed” which was an idea rejected by the American Psychiatric Association in 1973\‚ and now widely condemned by the American Psychological Association and World Health Organization for causing real psychological harm.    The data is clear:  • It does not change orientation  • People who undergo it are 2–3x more likely to experience depression and suicidality  • Survivors report significantly higher rates of anxiety\‚ shame\‚ and long term trauma    Jesus never coerced anything. He led with love. He restored dignity. He moved toward people and not away from them.    “By their fruit you will recognize them.” (Matthew 7:16)    If something produces shame\‚ fear\‚ and harm…we should be asking why we are contemplating this even to begin with.     👉 follow @spiritoutspoken to rediscover Jesus through history\‚ science and mysticism    #deconstruction #exvangelical #conversiontherapy #lgbtq

body: May Day is magical! Beltane is a pagan festival with  deep roots in Gaelic traditions. Now it is celebrated on May 1 by pagans\‚ witches\‚ and everyone who wants to feel closer to nature’s cycles. Beltane was dedicated to the beginning of summer: as a midpoint between the Spring Equinox and the Summer Solstice.    This year’s guide to Beltane is longer and renewed\‚ I’ve reworked it a lot—for instance\‚ adding multicultural perspective\‚ and deities to honor at this point of the year.     And\‚ of course\‚ we’ll see how we can celebrate it today\‚ my dear magical beings!👆🏼👉🏼    Have a majestic Beltane❤️‍🔥  With love and a bit of subtle sorcery\‚   Lida @lidapavlova_magic
